name: Jewish Caponata
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Tomatoes, ripe, peeled and cut up
1 cup Green olives, pitted, coarsely chopped
3 Basil leaves (fresh) OR 1 t Dried Basil
recipeInstructions:
1. To peel tomatoes, drop them into boiling water for about 1 minute, then immediately into cool water; the skin should slip off easily.
2. Peel and dice the eggplant. Season with salt and pepper and set aside to drain any liquid in a colander.
3. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over moderately high heat. Add celery, onion, peppers, garlic, and carrot. Cook, uncovered, stirring occasionally for 10 to 12 minutes.
4. Transfer vegetables to a shallow baking dish, leaving the oil in the skillet.
5. Add eggplant pieces to the hot oil in the skillet and sprinkle with flour. Fry, stirring, over moderate heat until lightly golden.
6. Place the fried eggplant into the baking dish with the other vegetables.
7. Add tomatoes, green olives, wine vinegar, sugar, basil (fresh or dried), and parsley to the dish. Spread evenly.
8. Transfer the mixture to a preheated oven set at 350°F (175°C) and bake for half an hour.
9. Remove from the oven; taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed. Stir well.
10. Return the dish to the oven for a couple of minutes longer, then add capers and give everything another stir.
11. Serve hot as a side dish or cold as an appetizer.
Serves: 6 as a side dish or 12 as an appetizer
